title: helianthemum canum subsp. stevenii
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. stevenii, also known as rockrose, is a plant species that belongs to the family Cistaceae and is native to the southwestern part of the United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral agent, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum canum subsp. stevenii include its potential to reduce the severity of colds and flu, alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. Helianthemum canum subsp. stevenii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
